title: The allelic spectrum of neutrality
author: OpenDiscover
blurb: Sample 50 genes at a neutral locus and ask: how do they fall into allele classes? Ewens' 1972 sampling formula answers exactly, from a single number θ = 4Nμ. Most alleles are singletons (seen once), a few are common — a lopsided spectrum that is the fingerprint of pure drift and mutation with no selection. Expected distinct alleles E[K] = Σ θ/(θ+i); expected homozygosity 1/(1+θ). Deviations from this curve are how the Ewens–Watterson test detects selection in real data.
